issue_claim: Admin can set unsafe slippage and cause poor swap execution
why_it_looked_bad: Excessive slippage can obviously harm execution quality and may appear to expose user funds to avoidable loss.
why_not_valid: The issue depends primarily on trusted admin misconfiguration rather than a permissionless exploit path. In the ordinary trust model this is an operational risk, not a submission-ready HM finding.
trust_assumption: the admin role is trusted to configure swap parameters safely
when_it_could_be_real: if an untrusted role can change slippage configuration
if user funds are forcibly routed through admin-controlled execution without user-set protection
if config updates can be front-run into user execution paths
